bead.title: routing: align score components with SD-005 evidence names
bead.description:
CONTEXT
The routing engine already scores with power, cost, quota, locality, utilization, performance, context headroom, cooldown, and sticky affinity. SD-005 names the public evidence components as `power_weighted_capability`, `power_hint_fit`, `latency_weight`, `placement_bonus`, `quota_bonus`, `marginal_cost_penalty`, `availability_penalty`, and `stale_signal_penalty`. Today those facts are present indirectly through buckets such as `power`, `cost`, `quota_health`, and `performance`, which makes route explanations harder to compare to the design.

CHANGE
Align public route candidate score component evidence with SD-005 vocabulary while preserving existing route-order behavior unless a test explicitly documents a deliberate change. Keep typed filter reasons separate from scoring. Power hints must remain asymmetric: being below `MinPower` is penalized more strongly than being above `MaxPower`.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- internal/routing/score.go
- internal/routing/*score*_test.go or routing preference tests
- service_routing.go candidate component mapping
- service_events.go if event JSON mirrors component names

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Snapshot assembly.
- Rewriting eligibility gates.
- Changing public request field names.
bead.acceptance:
1. `go test ./internal/routing/... -run "Score|Power|Preference" -count=1` passes with tests asserting score components sum to candidate score.
2. `go test . -run "TestRoutingDecision.*Components|TestRouteCandidate.*Components" -count=1` passes and verifies public evidence includes SD-005 component names.
3. A focused test proves below-MinPower penalty is greater per power point than above-MaxPower penalty.
4. Existing golden/behavioral route-order tests continue to pass, or any changed ordering is documented in test names and bead notes.
